metadata.title: Claude, On the Pitch — bot vs bot, season one · 6/7
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity UI beats; exhibits keep their own look (MUSINIQUE.md graded-exhibit law).
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Swedish (one-word budget — Musinique persona). Wagwan stays Bear's.
metadata.charter: MUSINIQUE.md — fights for the indie musician; receipts law; roll-your-own law; no fake numbers
metadata.note: ILLUSTRATE LAW: UI at B00 (with RESULT lines) / verdict / handoff / outro only; each inner beat carries an illustration spec in props.illustration (kind: concept | pattern | code | exhibit) — build per medhavy_com/youtube/mcp/remotion-src worked example + skills/animated-deck patterns. ROLL-YOUR-OWN LAW: the handoff paste is a deck piece the viewer keeps. RECEIPTS LAW: claims trace to RECEIPTS.md beside the playlist; illustrative examples labeled as such.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Sync placement is the money most indies never touch — not because the songs aren't good, but because the homework is brutal. Who placed what, where, for whom, in the last eighteen months. That homework is exactly machine-shaped. The handshake is not. We split it accordingly.

props.command: find every supervisor who placed songs like mine?
output: pitches drafted against your actual catalog

beats.narration_text: The overnight recipe: map the supervisors. What they placed, on which shows, how recently, how close to your lane. This used to be a week of tabs. It's now a night of your laptop staying awake. The brief you wake up to is a target list with reasons — not a purchased email dump from a 'sync opportunities' seller.

illustration.spec: Night-run illustration: placement cards assembling into a supervisor map — show, network, genre, recency

beats.narration_text: Then the draft — and the word draft is load-bearing. A pitch that names their actual placement and your actual song, in your voice file from episode four. What it never does is send. A named human being on the other end means your hand on the button. Episode two's red line, still holding.

illustration.spec: A pitch email card citing a specific placement + a specific song of yours; a DRAFT watermark over the whole card

beats.narration_text: Same machinery, same week: the non-musical mountain. The bio at three lengths. The press release. The booking email you've rewritten from scratch eleven times. This is the work indies skip — and skipping it is what keeps a career at hobby scale. Draft it all in one pass, in your voice, and spend the saved hours on the songs.

illustration.spec: The 'non-musical mountain' — bio (3 lengths), press release, EPK, booking email — each drafted and stacked into a road case

beats.narration_text: Hold your map next to what the scammers sell. 'Guaranteed placements.' 'Direct supervisor access.' Money up front, promises after, receipts never. You just built the real version overnight for the cost of electricity. The difference between you and their customer is one episode of this season.

illustration.spec: Split frame: 'guaranteed placement' service card (money in, promises out, no receipts) vs your overnight map (receipts, zero dollars)

beats.narration_text: One discipline before any pitch goes out: the placement claims in your brief pass the episode-three gate. If the machine says a supervisor placed a song on a show, that's checkable — check it. Citing a wrong placement to the person who'd know is how a door closes forever. No source, no sentence.

illustration.spec: A claim card ('placed on [show]') passing through the source-tier gate from episode 3 before it enters a pitch

beats.narration_text: One page. The machine does the week of tabs. You do the ten minutes that decide whether the door opens. That's the division of labor the promo sellers hope you never learn.

props.artifactTitle: Claude, On the Pitch — one page
artifactLines: Overnight: supervisors mapped by real, recent placements near your lane — reasons attached.
Drafted: pitches naming their placement and your song, in your voice file. DRAFT is load-bearing.
You send. Named humans never get machine-sent mail under your name.
Same pass drafts the mountain: bios, press release, EPK, booking mail. Verify every placement claim before it ships.

beats.narration_text: Your turn — schedule this one. Wake up to the map, spot-check three claims against the gate, send the two pitches you believe. Deck piece five: the pitch machine, with your hand on the trigger.

props.command: Overnight recipe: research music supervisors who placed songs comparable to mine in the last 18 months — show, network, genre, recency, with a source I can check for every placement claim. Draft one pitch per supervisor referencing their specific placement and the closest song in my catalog, in my brand voice. Mark every draft DO-NOT-SEND; I review and send each one myself.

beats.narration_text: Claude, on the pitch. Finale next: who signs — and why the whole deck is yours. Musinique.

props.title: Claude, On the Pitch.
props.subline: the machine hunts · you shake the hand
